title: Let's Go! Transportation Words (K-2)
description: A vocabulary pack for K-2 newcomer ELL students focusing on common transportation methods, featuring visual aids, a mini-book, and engaging worksheets.

vocabulary.image_prompt: A simple red car driving on a road.
A yellow school bus with children waving inside.
A child riding a blue bicycle with training wheels.
A colorful train with a happy face driving on tracks.
A white airplane flying high in a blue sky with clouds.
A small red boat sailing on calm blue water.
A person riding a black motorcycle on a road.
A yellow taxi cab with a 'TAXI' sign on top.
A bright red fire truck with a ladder and flashing lights.
A white ambulance with a red cross symbol.
A blue helicopter with spinning blades flying in the sky.

worksheets.instructions_en: Match the transportation word to its picture. Draw a line.
worksheets.instructions_l2: Une la palabra de transporte con su dibujo. Dibuja una línea.

worksheets.instructions_en: Color the pictures and say the word.
worksheets.instructions_l2: Colorea los dibujos y di la palabra.

items.image_prompt: An outline of a car to color.
An outline of a bus to color.
An outline of a bicycle to color.
An outline of a train to color.

pages.image_prompt: A collage of different transportation vehicles: car, bus, plane, boat.
A red car driving on a road.
A child riding a blue bicycle.
An airplane flying in the sky.

pages.text_l2: Un barco navega en el agua.

pages.image_prompt: A question mark surrounded by small images of various transportation.

teacher_notes.objective: Students will be able to identify and name at least 8 common modes of transportation in English and Spanish.
teacher_notes.suggested_use: Introduce vocabulary with flashcards and realia. Read the mini-book aloud, pointing to pictures. Have students repeat words. Use worksheets for practice and reinforcement. Encourage students to share their favorite transportation.
materials_needed: Optional: Toy vehicles for hands-on exploration
differentiation_tips: **For emerging ELLs:** Provide strong visual support (pictures, gestures). Focus on receptive vocabulary (pointing to the correct picture when named). Use sentence frames like 'It is a ____.'
**For developing ELLs:** Encourage speaking in short sentences. Pair with a more proficient student for peer support during activities. Use 'I see a ____.' or 'I like the ____.'
**For all students:** Incorporate total physical response (TPR) activities, e.g., 'Pretend to drive a car,' 'Fly like a plane.' Sing a transportation song.
